title: Hollywood Actor Hans Obma Joins Welsh Soap After Learning the Language
summary: Hans Obma, an American actor known for roles in Better Call Saul, The Vampire Diaries, and Criminal Minds, is joining the cast of the Welsh-language soap opera Pobl y Cwm. Inspired by his Welsh grandmother from Brynmawr, Obma learned Welsh to take on the role. He describes the experience as exhilarating and says Wales has become his second home. The show, which first aired in 1974 and moved to S4C in 1982, is 97% in Welsh. Obma plays an American character, so he retains his accent. He prepared by watching Welsh TV programs including Pobl y Cwm, Rownd a Rownd, and Newyddion S4C. Obma's grandmother moved to the US after World War Two and never spoke Welsh, but her accent returned when she visited Wales with him. Obma's addition to the cast highlights the show's ongoing appeal and its role in promoting the Welsh language.

background_read: Pobl y Cwm is the BBC's longest-running soap opera, having started in 1974. It moved to the Welsh-language channel S4C in 1982 and airs three times a week. The show is set in the fictional village of Cwmderi and has featured many famous Welsh actors. Learning Welsh can be challenging, but Obma found watching Welsh TV helpful.
Article_Structure: Main Points: A Hollywood actor joins a Welsh soap after learning the language, motivated by his Welsh heritage. Purpose: To report on an interesting cultural crossover and promote the Welsh language. Evidence Evaluation: The article includes direct quotes from the actor and factual details about the show, providing credible information. Author Credibility: The BBC is a reputable news source. Methodology: The information was gathered through an interview with the actor and background research on the show.

perspectives.description: Obma finds learning and using Welsh exhilarating and feels a strong connection to Wales through his grandmother.
The story highlights the importance of preserving minority languages and the role of media in language learning.
